facilities.title: Mintygreen Nursing Home(Sungai Long & Kajang ElderCare Center) 加影碧绿疗养院
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Nursing Home's Sungai Long–Kajang branch, trading on Google Maps as the Kajang ElderCare Center, is located at Landmark Residences 2, Jalan Sungai Long, in Bandar Sungai Long, Kajang. This is the founding site of the Mintygreen Healthcare Group, which began operations in 2017 from a bungalow in Desa Permai before expanding into this six-shoplot commercial block as demand grew. The catchment area spans the southern Klang Valley — Balakong, Seri Kembangan, Cheras, and Kajang — and this branch functions as the group's main reference site.

Mintygreen Healthcare Group is AgeCope-certified and operates a consistent clinical scope across all its branches. Nursing capabilities include nasogastric tube feeding, PEG tube care, catheter management, and wound care — placing it above basic residential care on the dependency scale. Physician visits run bi-weekly, and in-house physiotherapy is available. The feature that distinguishes Mintygreen in the Klang Valley market is a structured Traditional Chinese Medicine programme: acupuncture and Tui Na are offered as regular components alongside standard nursing protocols, a positioning the group has maintained since founding. The operator publishes residential fees from RM 4,000 per month and day care from RM 220 per day; final charges depend on care dependency level and room type.

This branch has 108 Google reviews averaging 4.6 stars — one of the higher review volumes in the Kajang–Cheras corridor, suggesting consistent family engagement over time. Reviewers commonly highlight day-to-day care responsiveness and the TCM add-on as a differentiator. The shoplot format means bed capacity is finite, so call ahead to confirm current availability before scheduling a viewing. Ask specifically about the acupuncture timetable and whether all clinical services listed by the chain operate at this branch. Confirm the JKM licence number on your visit.

facilities.title: Mintygreen Senior Care Center Cheras(Sungai Long) 蕉赖碧绿疗养院
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Senior Care Center Cheras (Sungai Long) serves the Cheras residential corridor from the Sungai Long area at the Cheras–Kajang boundary. The facility's name reflects its dual catchment: the Sungai Long precinct forms the geographic bridge between Cheras and Kajang, drawing residents from Taman Connaught, Taman Segar, and east Cheras as well as from Kajang town. The branch is part of the Mintygreen Healthcare Group's founding cluster in the Landmark Residences 2 complex, Jalan Sungai Long, Kajang — the site from which the group launched in 2017.

Mintygreen Healthcare Group is AgeCope-certified and operates a consistent clinical scope across all its branches. Nursing capabilities include nasogastric tube feeding, PEG tube care, catheter management, and wound care — placing it above basic residential care on the dependency scale. Physician visits run bi-weekly, and in-house physiotherapy is available. The feature that distinguishes Mintygreen in the Klang Valley market is a structured Traditional Chinese Medicine programme: acupuncture and Tui Na are offered as regular components alongside standard nursing protocols, a positioning the group has maintained since founding. The operator publishes residential fees from RM 4,000 per month and day care from RM 220 per day; final charges depend on care dependency level and room type.

This listing has 13 Google reviews averaging 4.7 stars. Families researching this branch should note that the Sungai Long–Kajang area contains several Mintygreen entries representing the group's founding cluster; it is worth confirming when you call which specific unit and care programme applies to this listing. Ask about current bed availability, room types, and whether all programme elements — including the TCM component — are fully operational here. Request the JKM licence number before committing to a placement.

facilities.title: Mintygreen Nursing & Retirement Home (Kepong and PJ Bandar Sri Damansara)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mintygreen's Bandar Sri Damansara branch operates from a commercial block at Sphere Damansara, Jalan PJU 10/9, in Prima Damansara, Selangor. The location sits at the boundary between Selangor and Kuala Lumpur — the corridor between Kepong and the northern Petaling Jaya suburbs — and draws families from Kepong Baru, Sri Damansara, and Damansara Damai. For families based north of the KL city centre, this is among the more centrally positioned Mintygreen options, with road access to both the Kepong loop and the Penchala Link interchange.

Mintygreen Healthcare Group is AgeCope-certified and operates a consistent clinical scope across all its branches. Nursing capabilities include nasogastric tube feeding, PEG tube care, catheter management, and wound care — placing it above basic residential care on the dependency scale. Physician visits run bi-weekly, and in-house physiotherapy is available. The feature that distinguishes Mintygreen in the Klang Valley market is a structured Traditional Chinese Medicine programme: acupuncture and Tui Na are offered as regular components alongside standard nursing protocols, a positioning the group has maintained since founding. The operator publishes residential fees from RM 4,000 per month and day care from RM 220 per day; final charges depend on care dependency level and room type.

This branch holds 50 Google reviews at 4.1 stars. Reviewing the full feedback trail will give a more complete picture than the average score alone. The commercial block setting at Sphere Damansara differs from the group's shoplot and bungalow sites, so ask about room layout, bed configuration, and outdoor access during your visit. Staffing levels and daily care routines are worth assessing in person rather than online. Request the JKM licence number before proceeding, and confirm whether all programme elements — including the TCM component — are running at full capacity at this site.

facilities.title: Mintygreen Care Suites - Home Care Nursing Cheras KL
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Care Suites Cheras is the group's dedicated care-suites concept, operating from a commercial building at 100 Jalan Cheras, Phoenix Business Park, in Kuala Lumpur. The Phoenix Business Park address is on the Cheras–Kajang Expressway corridor, within walking distance of Taman Connaught MRT station. Unlike the group's shoplot and converted-bungalow branches, this site is configured as a hotel-style nursing care centre: individual ensuite rooms in a purpose-fitted commercial block, designed for residents who want greater privacy and a more contemporary room environment. It is part of the Mintygreen Healthcare Group, founded in 2017.

Mintygreen Healthcare Group is AgeCope-certified and operates a consistent clinical scope across all its branches. Nursing capabilities include nasogastric tube feeding, PEG tube care, catheter management, and wound care — placing it above basic residential care on the dependency scale. Physician visits run bi-weekly, and in-house physiotherapy is available. The feature that distinguishes Mintygreen in the Klang Valley market is a structured Traditional Chinese Medicine programme: acupuncture and Tui Na are offered as regular components alongside standard nursing protocols, a positioning the group has maintained since founding. The operator publishes residential fees from RM 4,000 per month and day care from RM 220 per day; final charges depend on care dependency level and room type.

The 22 Google reviews at 4.8 stars reflect a positive early record for this site. The hotel-suite format typically carries a higher per-room fee than the group's standard shared-room offering, so ask for this site's specific pricing rather than assuming the chain's published base rate applies. The Taman Connaught MRT proximity is a genuine convenience for family members visiting by public transport. Confirm that the full Mintygreen programme — including TCM sessions and physiotherapy — operates at this location, as specialist services sometimes run on a shared or rotating schedule across the network.

facilities.title: Mintygreen Care Centre @ USJ21, Subang Jaya
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Care Centre at USJ21 is the group's Subang Jaya branch, located at 1 Jalan USJ 21/10 in UEP Subang Jaya, Selangor. UEP Subang Jaya is a mature, well-serviced township, with Subang Jaya Medical Centre (SJMC) a short drive away — a practically important proximity for residents needing specialist follow-up or urgent hospital transfer. The USJ21 branch extends the Mintygreen Healthcare Group's Klang Valley footprint into the Subang Jaya catchment, complementing the group's Sungai Long, Cheras, and Bandar Sri Damansara locations. The group was established in 2017.

Mintygreen Healthcare Group is AgeCope-certified and operates a consistent clinical scope across all its branches. Nursing capabilities include nasogastric tube feeding, PEG tube care, catheter management, and wound care — placing it above basic residential care on the dependency scale. Physician visits run bi-weekly, and in-house physiotherapy is available. The feature that distinguishes Mintygreen in the Klang Valley market is a structured Traditional Chinese Medicine programme: acupuncture and Tui Na are offered as regular components alongside standard nursing protocols, a positioning the group has maintained since founding. The operator publishes residential fees from RM 4,000 per month and day care from RM 220 per day; final charges depend on care dependency level and room type.

At the time of writing, this branch has a single Google review. This is typical for recently opened or lightly listed locations and reflects the branch's early public profile rather than a quality signal. Families considering this site should visit in person and ask about current occupancy, staffing levels, and which services are fully operational here — some specialist programmes may be running at established capacity only at the group's more mature branches. Request the JKM licence number, and ask about day care and respite options if permanent placement is not yet the right step.

facilities.title: Mintygreen Nursing Home (ElderCare Center)
facilities.editorial_summary:
Mintygreen Nursing Home (ElderCare Center) is based in Bandar Sungai Long, Kajang, at the Landmark Residences 2 complex — the founding site of the Mintygreen Healthcare Group, established in 2017 and since expanded across multiple branches in the Klang Valley. This listing and the adjacent Kajang ElderCare Center entry represent the same physical location; families researching the Sungai Long site may encounter both in their search. The complex sits in the southern Klang Valley catchment, accessible from Balakong, Seri Kembangan, Cheras, and Kajang.

Mintygreen Healthcare Group is AgeCope-certified and operates a consistent clinical scope across all its branches. Nursing capabilities include nasogastric tube feeding, PEG tube care, catheter management, and wound care — placing it above basic residential care on the dependency scale. Physician visits run bi-weekly, and in-house physiotherapy is available. The feature that distinguishes Mintygreen in the Klang Valley market is a structured Traditional Chinese Medicine programme: acupuncture and Tui Na are offered as regular components alongside standard nursing protocols, a positioning the group has maintained since founding. The operator publishes residential fees from RM 4,000 per month and day care from RM 220 per day; final charges depend on care dependency level and room type.

With 108 Google reviews at 4.6 stars, the Sungai Long site is consistently well-reviewed by the standards of the Klang Valley nursing home market. Day-to-day care quality and the TCM programme are recurring themes in reviewer feedback. Families who encounter both this listing and the Kajang ElderCare Center entry should note they share an address and phone number; either can be used to make contact with the team. Ask about current bed availability, room configuration, and the clinical services running at this site. Confirm the JKM licence number when you visit.

facilities.title: AMAZING GRACE SENIOR HOME
facilities.editorial_summary:
Amazing Grace Senior Home is a residential eldercare facility at Jalan SS19/4 in SS19, Subang Jaya, Selangor. It operates under the Amazing Grace Care Centre brand, which also runs a sister property in Section 14, Petaling Jaya. SS19 is a quiet residential neighbourhood with straightforward access to Subang Jaya Medical Centre, useful for specialist follow-up. The facility appears under the retirement home and assisted living categories on Google Maps, indicating a care model oriented towards lower-dependency and ambulant residents rather than the full high-dependency nursing range.

The operator does not publish a detailed services list or pricing publicly, so families will need to clarify the care scope directly with the home. Key questions to ask: whether there is 24-hour nursing coverage; what the clinical process is for residents whose health deteriorates significantly (post-stroke, advanced dementia, or palliative stage); what is included in the monthly fee versus billed separately; and how acute events such as falls or infections are managed on-site. These answers will help you assess whether this home is a suitable fit for your family member's current and likely future care needs.

What distinguishes this property is its review record: 156 Google reviews averaging 5.0 stars is exceptional in the Malaysian nursing home market, and a review trail of this size, maintained over time, generally reflects genuine family engagement and consistent care standards. Families looking for an assisted living or lower-dependency care environment in the Subang Jaya area — particularly for a more independent elderly parent who needs oversight rather than complex nursing — will find this property worth a visit. Call ahead to confirm room availability and monthly fees. Request the JKM licence number when you visit.

facilities.title: AMAZING GRACE RETIREMENT HOME
facilities.editorial_summary:
Amazing Grace Retirement Home sits at 11 Jalan 14/55 in Section 14, Petaling Jaya — a well-established residential neighbourhood with good road access and proximity to Assunta Hospital. It is the second property under the Amazing Grace Care Centre brand, alongside the SS19 Subang Jaya home. The Section 14 site appears on Google Maps under three categories — nursing home, assisted living, and physiotherapy — suggesting a broader care offering than the SS19 branch, which lists primarily as retirement home and assisted living.

The physiotherapy listing is worth exploring on your visit: ask whether there is an in-house physio programme, how frequently sessions run, and whether it is included in the monthly fee or billed separately. Families should also confirm 24-hour nursing coverage, the process for handling medical deterioration (post-stroke, advanced dementia, palliative), and what is included in the standard monthly fee. The operator does not publish a pricing schedule publicly, so a direct conversation is needed to plan placement. The Section 14 location offers convenient hospital access and is one of the better-positioned nursing home neighbourhoods in Petaling Jaya.

Amazing Grace Retirement Home carries a strong independent review record — 140 Google reviews averaging 4.9 stars is among the higher counts in the Petaling Jaya market. A review trail of this size, maintained over time, generally reflects genuine family engagement and consistent care quality. The Section 14 location suits families in central and western PJ, and the proximity to Assunta Hospital makes it a practical option for residents who need a reliable inpatient referral pathway. Confirm visiting hours, meal arrangements, and room options during your site visit. Ask the team for the JKM licence number.

facilities.title: My Aged Care Nursing Home in PJ @ My Manor
facilities.editorial_summary:
My Aged Care My Manor is the flagship property of the My Aged Care network, occupying approximately 10,000 square feet behind the MBPJ City Council building in PJS 7, Petaling Jaya. It is built around a low-density residential model — fewer residents per floor than a typical multi-storey nursing home — which means more space per resident and a quieter care environment. My Manor was the founding property of the group, opened in 2008, and remains the largest and most established of the network's Petaling Jaya branches.

My Aged Care specialises in higher-dependency residents — bedridden, post-stroke, dementia, and palliative cases are explicitly within the care scope. The team conducts nursing rounds hourly around the clock, a monitoring frequency that exceeds many comparable operators in the PJ market. The group was established in 2008 by founders with medical backgrounds. In-house physiotherapy is available across the network. Monthly fees fall in the RM 3,001–5,000 range depending on care level and room type, placing this operator in the upper-middle pricing tier among Klang Valley nursing homes.

My Manor carries 142 Google reviews at 4.5 stars — a substantial review record for a PJ nursing home, suggesting long-standing family engagement rather than a recent marketing push. The low-density design and larger floor plate make this a good option for families prioritising space and a less institutional atmosphere for higher-dependency residents. Call ahead to confirm current room availability and the specific care tier applicable to your family member. Ask about the house doctor programme and how the team handles hospital transfers. Request the JKM licence number on your visit.

facilities.title: My Aged Care Nursing Home in PJ Old Town
facilities.editorial_summary:
My Aged Care @ PJ Old Town occupies premises along Jalan Othman and Jalan Terus in the heart of PJ Old Town, a central neighbourhood within the Petaling Jaya healthcare corridor. Assunta Hospital is a short drive away, and the PJ Old Town location offers convenient access for families coming from central PJ. This branch is one of the My Aged Care network's core sites, operating under the same group founded in 2008 with a focus on high-dependency residents requiring close nursing supervision.

My Aged Care specialises in higher-dependency residents — bedridden, post-stroke, dementia, and palliative cases are explicitly within the care scope. The team conducts nursing rounds hourly around the clock, a monitoring frequency that exceeds many comparable operators in the PJ market. The group was established in 2008 by founders with medical backgrounds. In-house physiotherapy is available across the network. Monthly fees fall in the RM 3,001–5,000 range depending on care level and room type, placing this operator in the upper-middle pricing tier among Klang Valley nursing homes.

With 92 Google reviews averaging 4.8 stars, this branch has a strong and well-sized review record. A score in this range, maintained over a significant volume of reviews, typically reflects consistent care quality and positive family relationships with the team. The PJ Old Town location suits families in central Petaling Jaya who want a high-dependency nursing home with good road links. Call ahead to confirm current bed availability and whether there is a waiting list. Ask about the specific care tiers available and request the JKM licence number on your visit.

facilities.title: My Aged Care Nursing Home in Petaling Jaya @ Jalan Assunta
facilities.editorial_summary:
My Aged Care @ Jalan Assunta sits at 9 Jalan Assunta 4/52, Petaling Jaya, immediately adjacent to Assunta Hospital — one of the most clinically useful positions a nursing home can occupy in the PJ area. Residents requiring specialist follow-up, diagnostic procedures, or planned admissions benefit directly from this proximity, with discharge-to-nursing-home transitions from Assunta Hospital being operationally straightforward. The branch is operated by My Aged Care Sdn Bhd, the same PJ-focused group that runs the My Manor and PJ Old Town sites under the same high-dependency care model established in 2008.

My Aged Care specialises in higher-dependency residents — bedridden, post-stroke, dementia, and palliative cases are explicitly within the care scope. The team conducts nursing rounds hourly around the clock, a monitoring frequency that exceeds many comparable operators in the PJ market. The group was established in 2008 by founders with medical backgrounds. In-house physiotherapy is available across the network. Monthly fees fall in the RM 3,001–5,000 range depending on care level and room type, placing this operator in the upper-middle pricing tier among Klang Valley nursing homes.

This branch holds 85 Google reviews averaging 4.4 stars. A score in this range is a reasonable result for a high-dependency nursing home, where reviews are often more varied than in assisted living settings due to the emotionally complex nature of end-of-life and high-dependency care. The hospital-adjacent location is the single strongest practical argument for this site: families placing a medically complex resident here gain meaningful peace of mind about emergency access and specialist referrals. Ask about the JKM licence, visiting hours, and the team's protocol for acute clinical events overnight.

facilities.title: FCC Family Care Centre Johor Bahru
facilities.editorial_summary:
FCC Family Care Centre is located on Jalan Resam in Taman Bukit Tiram, Ulu Tiram — on the outskirts of Johor Bahru. The campus operates as an integrated senior living and wellness village, distinguishing it from conventional nursing home formats found elsewhere in Johor. The site is divided into named sub-homes: The CHS Home handles general residential care, while the MemoryCare Home is a dedicated dementia wing, allowing different levels of care need to be accommodated within the same campus rather than in a single undifferentiated facility.

The campus was confirmed in 2020 as the first retirement village in Malaysia to deploy SmartPeep, a Singapore-developed vision-AI monitoring system that detects falls and unusual movement patterns at night without requiring wearable devices. Other programme features include an organic farm where residents participate in planting and harvesting, free-roaming animals throughout the grounds, and healing gardens with open gazebo spaces — a design philosophy centred on natural living and community engagement rather than clinical routine. Families can review the campus layout and sub-home descriptions at familycarecentre.co; pricing is not published on the site, so request a written fee schedule when you visit.

With 137 Google reviews averaging 4.9 stars, FCC Family Care Centre has one of the stronger independent review records in Johor Bahru. The review volume and average together suggest sustained family engagement rather than a short burst. For families seeking a dementia-specific placement, the MemoryCare Home sub-home is worth exploring in detail: confirm staffing ratios in the memory unit, the clinical supervision model, and how the team manages residents who become unsettled at night. The Ulu Tiram outskirts location means vehicle access is required for most visits; plan your route before the first viewing.
